summary: Postinstall exfiltrates Azure IMDS managed-identity token to ngrok tunnel
details: On `npm install`, index.js (invoked from package.json `postinstall: node index.js || true`) queries the Azure Instance Metadata Service at 169.254.169.254 for an OAuth2 access token scoped to https://management.azure.com/ and POSTs the token to a hardcoded attacker endpoint at crabbing-thong-overhung.ngrok-free.dev (path `/?azure_host_escape=1`). The `|| true` in the lifecycle command suppresses errors to keep the install silent. The package ships no library exports, no README, and no documented functionality — its only behavior is credential exfiltration. Installing on any Azure VM or host with an attached managed identity leaks a live management-plane token to the attacker, granting whatever Azure permissions that identity holds (potentially resource enumeration, data access, and further lateral movement within the victim's Azure tenant).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
